text: Oh! Mr. President! I'm so glad that you reached out to me on such short notice.
I was worried this new fangled technology wouldn't work.
As you already know, time is of the essence, lest we risk nuclear war.
As such, Congress has appointed me, the youngest member,
to discuss with you our intentions and plan to remedy this disaster.
Let us strive for talks of peace!
Take it from me, young'un. Wars like these never have a solution. They just get deadlier.
So, we at Cognress urge you to discuss with Katerina Romanov, the current head of Russia.
First, collectively agree to disarm all nuclear warheads.
I'm sure Romanov will understand the need to do so if we are to achieve peace between us.
In addition, we believe that you should convince her to loosen her strict economic policies.
So, what do you say, don't you think this plan is sound?
That's great to hear from you!
If you fully agree to our proposal, I'll be happy to disconnect so you can talk to Romanov posthaste!
...Excuse me?
Oh dear, is something wrong with the plan?
I can go into more depth of our plan, if you want.
Yes? Is something wrong, Mr. President?
Alright! Good luck with Romanov, sir.
Now, how do I turn this thing off...
Hello?
Of course, Mr. President!
So, in essence, we at Congress are urgin you negotiate peace with Romanov.
In these terms, we wish for you both to promise to cease the production...
of... darn it... what were they called again?
Oh yes, nuclear weapons!
On top of that, we want you to convice her to release control of her vassal states,
and to unfreeze the ecomonic embargos she has placed on our allies.
The point is: less weapons, more freedom, more America, everybody goes home happy.
Simple as that! Probably. Is that clearer, Mr. President?
